channel.title: Chroniq Now: Breaking Global News and Headlines Daily
channel.description: Chroniq Now - your trusted source for breaking global news, in-depth analysis and up-to-the-minute coverage of politics, business, culture and more.

item.title: SEC Proposes Ending Mandatory Quarterly Earnings Reports
item.description:
The SEC has proposed making quarterly earnings filings optional for U.S. public companies, potentially allowing firms to shift to semiannual reporting in what regulators are framing as a direct strike against corporate short-termism.

Key points:
• Companies could opt to file one semiannual report (on a new Form 10-S) plus one annual report, replacing the current three quarterly 10-Qs and one annual report
• The move would end a 55-year-old requirement dating to 1970, bringing the U.S. closer to reporting norms in Europe and the UK
• SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the current rules prevent companies and investors from choosing the reporting frequency that best fits their needs
• The proposal enters a 60-day public comment period before the SEC votes on it; quarterly reporting would remain an option for companies that prefer it

Read the full article: SEC Proposes Ending Mandatory Quarterly Earnings Reports

item.title: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
item.description:
Driven Advisors and Administrative Services face scrutiny in a legal dispute with Bancrédito Holding Corp, owner of Bancrédito International Bank and Trust.

Read the full article: Driven Advisors Scrutinized: Bancrédito Holding Dispute
